methodsAttempted.notes: Fetched robots.txt from the official University of Balamand domain; only /it/ was disallowed, so the English policy and PDF paths used here were allowed.

methodsAttempted.query: site:balamand.edu.lb (AI OR "artificial intelligence" OR ChatGPT OR "generative AI" OR "academic integrity") "University of Balamand"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official AI course, certificate, news, department, lecture, and research pages. These were treated as discovery leads, not policy evidence, unless they contained substantive official policy language.

methodsAttempted.query: site:balamand.edu.lb "academic integrity" "University of Balamand" policy students
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official Current Students policy index, the Academic Integrity and Copyright Policy PDF, the Student Handbook, catalogue sections, and the Ethical Use of IT Policy PDF.

methodsAttempted.query: University of Balamand official policy pages for academic integrity, copyright, information technology, artificial intelligence, and data mining
methodsAttempted.notes: Official-domain checks verified the Academic Integrity and Copyright PDF and Ethical Use of IT PDF as policy sources. AI certificate, course, partnership, and lecture pages were rejected as low-specificity or non-policy materials.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found no standalone University of Balamand generative-AI or ChatGPT policy. The usable official policy evidence was limited to the Academic Integrity and Copyright Policy PDF and the Ethical Use of Information Technology Policy PDF. AI-specific official pages found in search were courses, certificates, news, or research/showcase pages and were recorded as rejected for policy-evidence use.

The Ethical Use of Information Technology Policy

artifacts.claimText: University of Balamand students are responsible for the integrity of submitted coursework; the Academic Integrity and Copyright Policy says students must do their own work unless the instructor asks otherwise, may collaborate on graded work only if instructed to do so, and must follow course requirements specified in the syllabus.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Balamand Academic Integrity and Copyright Policy treats plagiarism as passing off another person's work as one's own and lists examples including undocumented text, quotations without quotation marks, undocumented paraphrase or summary, untranslated text without citation, and packaged online information or completed papers submitted without acknowledgment.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Academic Integrity and Copyright Policies for the University of Balamand
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students assume full responsibility for the content and integrity of the coursework they submit. The following are guidelines to assist students in observing academic integrity: Students must do their own work and submit only their own work on examinations, reports, and projects, unless otherwise asked by the instructor. Students may collaborate or cooperate with other students on graded assignments or examinations if instructed to do so by the instructor.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students are responsible for submitted coursework, must do their own work unless asked otherwise, and may collaborate on graded work only when instructed.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Academic Integrity Policy, section 1.2 Guidelines for Academic Integrity
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Academic Integrity and Copyright Policies for the University of Balamand
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Academic Integrity and Copyright Policies for the University of Balamand
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Plagiarism is intentionally or carelessly passing off another person's work as one's own. It is taking and using information, ideas, opinions, theories or another person's actual words or source without acknowledging the source, thereby creating the impression that the work is one's own. Plagiarism can occur in the following ways: Using text from another source... without documenting the source... Obtaining packaged information, foreign language translation or a completed paper from an on-line source and submitting it as one's own work without acknowledgment of the source.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The policy defines plagiarism as using another person's work or source without acknowledgment, including text from online sources and packaged online information.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Academic Integrity Policy, section 1.3 Forms of Academic Dishonesty
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.title: University of Balamand AI Policy Source Review

limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.

No binding generative-AI-specific University of Balamand policy was found in official-domain discovery during this run; AI courses, certificates, news, and showcase pages were not used as policy evidence.
